music.name: Bay-Tek 2026: Perc, Foor, Just Jovani, Miss Crafty, Moonpie, Papa Xanny
music.why: A DIY river-delta camping mission for techno heads who want a real expedition, not a club night
music.summary: A first-edition multi-day techno festival on a floating island in the Sacramento River Delta, July 3-6, with day and night stages, swimming, and car/RV camping. Reached only by ferry, private boat, or your own kayak.

music.name: ILLUM Block Party: deadmau5, Nero, Cristoph b2b Rebuke, Maneki
music.why: Mainstage names without the late-night grind, wrapped by 10pm; the marquee daytime splurge of the weekend
music.summary: An outdoor afternoon block party at The Midway in SF on Saturday, running 3-10pm, with a headliner-stacked house and electronic bill spread across the venue's open-air yard over the July 4th weekend.

music.name: Stern Grove Festival: Major Lazer with Fijiana
music.address: 19th Ave & Sloat Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94132
music.why: Free Major Lazer at a redwood amphitheater — bring a blanket and arrive early; the prestige free show of the weekend
music.summary: The Stern Grove Festival presents Major Lazer with Fijiana on Sunday July 5 at 2pm in Stern Grove (19th Ave & Sloat Blvd, SF), an outdoor natural amphitheater ringed by redwoods; free, all ages. Arrive early — seating fills fast.

music.name: Walshy Fire (Major Lazer) July 4th Holiday Rave
music.address: 2236 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Oakland, CA 94612
music.why: The weekend's dancehall and afrobeats pick; three rooms of Caribbean heat across the bay in Oakland
music.summary: A 21+ late-night Caribbean-style rave on July 4th, 10pm-4am, at Starline Social Club in Oakland, with a Major Lazer member headlining three rooms of dancehall, soca, afrobeats and global sounds.

music.name: After Life: Rebellion w/ Louisahhh
music.why: The 18+ goth-adjacent dark-techno option with a live vocal set; cathartic and heavy, not a goth party
music.summary: An 18+ dark electronica night on July 4th, 9:30pm-2am, above DNA Lounge in SF, with a live hybrid set from the headliner across two rooms spanning darkwave, industrial bass and dark techno.

music.name: BUNT. in the Sacramento Round
music.why: An 18+ daytime block party for the Sacramento crowd; melodic crossover EDM, easy entry age
music.summary: An 18+ outdoor EDM and house block party on Friday July 3 at 3pm in Ali Youssefi Square, Sacramento, presented by All Day All Night with a single headliner for an afternoon-into-evening crowd.

music.name: Elderbrook Day Party
music.address: 1 Avenue of the Palms, San Francisco, CA 94130
music.why: Live performance, not just a DJ, with the best SF skyline view of the weekend; take the ferry
music.summary: A 21+ open-air sunset day party on Friday July 3, 2-9pm at Gold Bar on Treasure Island, a 1939 Art Moderne former airport terminal, featuring a live house headliner with skyline views, food and drinks; ferry access available.

music.name: Goom Gum, Torteline, Thalia
music.why: Catch a buzzy duo's SF debut at an intimate room; melodic, driving and till 4am
music.summary: A 21+ melodic techno and progressive house show on Friday July 3, 10pm-4am, at Halcyon in SF, marking the headlining duo's SF debut on their first US tour with two supporting acts.

music.name: Jayda G Day Party
music.why: Sunday-afternoon rooftop house to ease out of the holiday; cheap entry and a daytime close
music.summary: A house and disco rooftop day party on Sunday July 5, 2-7pm, at 620 Jones in SF, hosted by MAKEROOM! with the headliner taking over the venue alongside support.

music.name: Nora En Pure (4th of July)
music.why: Melodic house in a graffitied factory ruin; the scenic Napa-edge day party for a sundown vibe
music.summary: A 21+ outdoor day party on July 4th at The Ruins, an abandoned 1900s cement factory 25 miles outside SF in American Canyon, running 3-9:30pm with a deep/melodic house headliner, support DJs, full bar and food trucks.

music.name: Pan-Pot, Dean Samaras, Just Jovani
music.why: Proper Berlin techno till 4am at a tight underground room; cheap entry for the all-nighter crowd
music.summary: A 21+ late-night techno and tech-house show on July 4th, 10pm-4am, at Halcyon in SF, headlined by the returning Berlin duo with two supporting DJs on the dance floor.

music.name: Shiba San
music.why: A French tech-house heavyweight doing a rare South Bay club date — not another SF warehouse line-up
music.summary: Tech-house star Shiba San headlines PURE Nightclub in Sunnyvale on Friday July 3. 21+, doors 10pm; lower ticket tiers were already selling out.

music.name: Todd Terje (DJ Set)
music.why: Sunny nu-disco grooves for the late slot; the feel-good club option, not heads-down techno
music.summary: A late-night disco and nu-disco DJ set on July 4th, 9pm-3am, at Public Works in SF, presented by DJ Dials in the club's main room over the holiday weekend.

music.name: Connect / Rave Grave / BounceSF: Zlata
music.why: Three collectives stacking a ten-deep lineup around ZLATA — the most loaded SF underground Friday of the weekend
music.summary: Connect, Rave Grave and BounceSF co-headline Friday July 3 at F8 in SF (1192 Folsom) at 9pm, with ZLATA headlining the main room above ELA MENTAL, JustJovani, Caspii, Lil Laurie, Jai and MNHTN; Rave Grave holds the backroom with Saladbar, Switch, Splash and Maka; free–$23.

music.name: Leah York: Noche de Perreo
music.why: Late perreo with the cheapest cover going; the all-night reggaeton call over the day parties
music.summary: A 21+ reggaeton and perreo night on July 4th, 9pm-3am, at F8 in SF, with the headliner leading a Latin dance party across the holiday weekend.

music.name: E-40 & Friends (4th of July Weekend)
music.why: Bay Area hip-hop royalty headlining a hometown July 4th day party — a locals-first block party feel, not a festival
music.summary: E-40 & Friends play a July 4th weekend day party at The Midway in SF (900 Marin St) on Sunday July 5, 4–10pm, with a Bay Area hip-hop lineup; $40–$121.

music.name: Fillmore Jazz Festival 2026
music.address: Fillmore St (Jackson to Eddy), San Francisco, CA 94115
music.why: The largest free jazz street fair on the West Coast returns along Fillmore St — no wristband, just show up
music.summary: The Fillmore Jazz Festival runs Saturday July 4 and Sunday July 5, 10am–6pm each day, along Fillmore Street from Jackson to Eddy in SF, with live jazz and blues stages, art, food and vendors across several blocks; free, all ages.

music.name: Aleroj + Isablu, maracuya, briiza, xtcemi
music.why: Colombian label Muakk meets SF underground: guaracha, footwork and hardcore in a secret warehouse — rare and sweaty
music.summary: Hazardous Nemesis presents Aleroj and Isablu (of Colombia's Muakk label) alongside Briizv, xtcemi and DJ Saratonin at a secret SF warehouse on Friday July 3, 10pm–4am; guaracha, footwork, juke, techno and hardcore; $10–$30.

music.name: Ascension: Wilson Chang, Nikademis, Home By Dawn, Yanebun
music.address: 418 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113
music.why: The South Bay's melodic-bass night; wear red, catch a debut, skip the drive into the city
music.summary: A 21+ melodic bass and dubstep night on July 4th, 9:30pm-2am, at Era Lounge in San Jose, with a multi-act lineup, a world debut, and a red dress code for the South Bay community.

music.name: JINJER: Duel North America 2026
music.why: Ukrainian progressive metal powerhouse on a major tour stop at The Fillmore — proper concert, not a club night
music.summary: JINJER plays The Fillmore in San Francisco (1805 Geary Blvd) on Friday July 3 at 7pm on the Duel North America 2026 tour; $60.

music.name: Kausmic Campout
music.why: Easy drive-in forest camping with a pool by day and stars by night; nature reset, not a club
music.summary: A 21+ multi-day camping festival July 3-5 on private land in the Sierra Mountains, three hours from SF, with daytime pool-party stages and a big open-air night stage spanning house, tech house, afro house and techno, plus art, sound baths and on-site showers.

music.name: My Bloody Valentine – Loveless (Envelop Immersive Listening)
music.why: Loveless played through 32-speaker spatial audio — the album the way it was meant to batter you
music.summary: Envelop SF presents an immersive listening session of My Bloody Valentine's Loveless on Monday June 29, with two screening times at 7:30pm and 9:30pm, at The Midway (Envelop SF space, 900 Marin St). The nonprofit's 32-speaker ambisonics system delivers the album in three-dimensional sound; no age restriction noted.

music.name: Pedroz Independence Day Boat Party
music.why: A floating house and techno party past the Bay Bridge on July 5th — fireworks on deck, nowhere else to be
music.summary: Pedroz hosts an Independence Day boat party departing China Basin Ferry Terminal (679 3rd St, SF) on Sunday July 5, 6pm–1am, with house and techno DJs aboard a vessel on the Bay; $66–$87. Presented by Halcyon SF.

music.name: Shrek Rave
music.why: Ironic themed bass rave with Shrek cosplay encouraged — the most unhinged fun of the holiday weekend
music.summary: The Shrek Rave returns to The Midway in SF (900 Marin St) on Friday July 3 at 9pm, with bass-heavy DJs and Shrek-themed cosplay; $27.

music.name: Dykeabolical: Mama San, Boyprincess
music.why: A dyke-takeover bass night with baile-funk, arm wrestling and dancers — specific, queer and loud
music.summary: Dykeabolical takes over There/There Bar in Oakland on Thursday July 2 at 7pm, with a special dyke arm-wrestling event from WISDOM, dancers Nala and Kisa, and DJs Mama San and Boyprincess playing baile-funk and Latin bass; $5–$20.

music.name: Solar and Galen (Strut SF)
music.why: Veteran SF house legends Solar and Galen play long extended sets — deep classic house on a Wednesday night, free
music.summary: Strut SF and F8 present Solar and Galen (Sunset Sound System) for extended headline sets on Wednesday July 1, with Ghenji support in the main room and Program Audio (Erika, SF Cowboy) in Room 2; free, at F8 SF (1192 Folsom), 9pm.

music.name: Champion (UK) – Bay Area Debut
music.why: South London UK funky/grime pioneer's Bay Area headline debut — collaborator of Four Tet and Skrillex, long overdue
music.summary: Champion (UK) makes his Bay Area headline debut at Space 550 in SF (550 Barneveld Ave) on Saturday July 4 at 10pm, with support from Nightware and Kaytree; 44 RA interested; $25–$37.

music.name: Death Guild
music.why: The world's oldest weekly goth-industrial night; Monday regulars only, no tourists, six DJs two rooms
music.summary: Death Guild is San Francisco's legendary Monday goth/industrial/synthpop night at DNA Lounge, running 9:30pm–2:30am on June 29. Six DJs across two rooms play gothic, industrial, synthpop and darkwave; 18+, $5 advance/$8 after 10:30pm.

music.name: Hardcore In The Bay: Ravers In The U.S.A.
music.why: Free happy-hardcore for the kandi kids; bring your UV fit, the most wholesome chaos of the weekend
music.summary: A free 21+ rave on Friday July 3, 9pm-2am, at BuzzWorks in SF, with five DJs spinning happy hardcore, hardstyle and eurodance for the Bay Area kandi community; free with RSVP.

music.name: Kandi Korps 4th of July Massive: Estellita, Moxbpm, MDMAngel, Ihaveabomb
music.why: Ten-dollar warehouse bass massive for the kandi crowd; cheapest deep-rave ticket of the weekend
music.summary: A bass and rave night on July 4th from 10pm at 3rd & Army in SF, billed as a holiday massive with a stacked kandi-scene DJ lineup and more acts to be announced.

music.name: Perreo Eléctrico: Latin Rave
music.why: Live band plus DJ perreo at DNA Lounge — underground Latin rave pushing corrido and reggaeton on the pre-July 4th night
music.summary: Perreo Eléctrico: Latin Rave at DNA Lounge in SF (375 11th St) on Friday July 3, 9:30pm–2:30am, with DJ OtebNSolrac and live performance from Royales spanning techno, banda, house, corrido and reggaeton; 18+, $10 limited advance / $15 after / $22 door.

music.name: Music Is The Answer: Hidra & friends
music.why: Free Thursday afro-house and deep house night at F8, headlined by Hidra with a packed supporting cast
music.summary: HIDRA and F8 present Music Is The Answer on Thursday July 2, 9pm–2am, with Hidra, Mode Leeloo, Kenny Cordeiro and additional acts across two rooms of afro-house and deep house; free, at F8 SF (1192 Folsom).

music.name: Little Dinosaur – Salty Residency
music.why: Napa Music Hall's Friday residency — serious house-and-techno DJ talent in a non-SF setting, worth the Napa drive
music.summary: Little Dinosaur headlines the Salty Residency at Napa Music Hall on Friday July 3, 9pm–2am, presented by Blaire and Tamer of Salty Sundays Productions; $28.

music.name: 360 Picnic: Munnday, Damfu, The Homie J, Pulse, JVXX, Devine
music.why: Free, family-friendly, blanket-and-cooler park hang that runs right into the city fireworks
music.summary: A free, all-ages outdoor July 4th picnic at Caldwell Park's Oak Knoll in Redding, 10am until the city fireworks, with all-day 360 DJ sets of house, UK garage and bass, plus games for friends and families.

music.name: 4Ney SF Debut / Bump 1 Year Party
music.why: Bump SF's one-year anniversary at Monarch, bringing 4Ney for their SF debut — a genuine community milestone night
music.summary: Bump SF celebrates one year of Thursdays at Monarch by hosting 4Ney's SF debut, on Thursday July 2, 8pm–2am, at Monarch Upstairs Lounge in SF (101 6th St); $11–$26.

music.name: Basement Bounce
music.why: Fast, sweaty, uptempo club music you won't find at the house parties
music.summary: A late-night jersey club, Miami bass and ghettotech party at Mr. Mahjong's in SF, kicking off at midnight Friday.

music.name: Betical at Madarae
music.why: Burning Man-favorite melodic house in a slick club; emotional, indie-leaning, not peak-time pounding
music.summary: A 21+ deep, melodic and afro house night on Friday July 3, 9pm-3am, at Madarae in SF, headlined by the Spanish brother duo behind the viral "Back on 74" rework, with bottle service and tables available.

music.name: Bootie Mashup: Party In The USA 2008–2016
music.why: Throwback Obama-era pop mashups with drag; pure nostalgic fun, zero underground pretension
music.summary: A 21+ pop mashup party on July 4th, 9pm-2am, at Cat Club in SF, with two rooms of Obama-era 2008-2016 mashups, drag and burlesque from The Monster Show, and music-video projections all night.

music.name: Club Necromancy
music.why: Dark-floor refuge for anyone allergic to July 4th flag-waving
music.summary: A goth, industrial and synthpop night at The Press Club in Sacramento on Saturday, 9pm–1:30am.

music.name: Cyclops
music.why: Techno multi-act at The Midway's main room on the Friday before July 4th — the sizable pre-holiday club option
music.summary: Cyclops brings a techno lineup to The Midway in SF (900 Marin St) on Friday July 3 at 9pm; $31.

music.name: Daft Disko: Independence Day
music.why: All French touch, all night; nostalgic disco-house for people who'd rather dance than rave
music.summary: A 21+ French house and disco party on July 4th, 9pm-2am, at Bella in SF, spinning the music of Daft Punk, Justice, Stardust and the wider French touch catalog for the holiday.

music.name: DJ Pool Party: DJ Crabhat, John Parker, Em Bass, Dyops +more
music.why: Free riverside pool day in redwood country — the low-key daytime antidote to a club-heavy weekend
music.summary: A free all-day poolside party at Rio Nido Roadhouse up the Russian River on Sunday, 11am–6pm, with rotating DJs across house, disco, funk and drum & bass in a riverside resort setting.

music.name: Glitch.Bass: Iterate, RËIA, warble, Agatron, DJ BINX, Chaiberpunk
music.why: Deep-cut glitch-hop bill for heads who want wonky bass over big-room EDM
music.summary: A glitch-hop and dubstep showcase at Broadway Studios in North Beach on Friday, 7pm–1am, stacking six local bass acts across the night.

music.name: Hag, Idnas, Xilenyia, Zuela
music.address: 72 N Almaden Ave, San Jose, CA 95110
music.why: South Bay daytime perreo you can walk into free if you show up early
music.summary: A Sunday afternoon-into-evening Latin club session at SP2 in downtown San Jose, 3–9pm, spanning reggaeton, hip-hop and EDM with free RSVP entry early.

music.name: Interdependance Day
music.why: An anti-nationalist July 4th all-dayer mixing live global sound with DJs — a values-forward alternative
music.summary: A 15-hour day-to-night gathering at The Loom in Oakland on Saturday, blending live bands and world music with house and afrobeats DJs as a July 4th alternative celebration.

music.name: Late Night Clubbing #054
music.why: For when last call is just the warm-up — pure after-hours
music.summary: An after-hours house and EDM session in SF running 2–6am into Saturday morning, picking up where the clubs leave off.

music.name: Lean On: Qaotiq, Lychy Jelly, From Dust (Stern Grove Warm-Up)
music.why: 2010s trap/future-bass throwback with a Major Lazer ticket giveaway — cheap Haight weekend kickoff
music.summary: The Faight Collective's official Stern Grove warm-up: a 2010s trap, future-bass and electronic-pop throwback on Friday July 3, 7–11pm (doors 6:30, 21+), with Qaotiq, Lychy Jelly and From Dust spinning golden-era anthems (Major Lazer, Flume, RL Grime). Includes a giveaway of tickets to Sunday's Major Lazer Stern Grove show.

music.name: Milpitas Pop-up
music.why: Free South Bay park hang where you can actually sign up to spin; fireworks included
music.summary: A free, all-ages outdoor open-decks pop-up on July 4th, 12-5pm, at Cardoza Park in Milpitas, with community DJs, a special guest from 5-9pm, and the city's free fireworks show after; bring blankets and snacks.

music.name: Perreo En La Mision
music.why: Free perreo in the heart of the Mission; the Latin-night alternative to the techno parties
music.summary: A free 21+ reggaeton, cumbia and Latin house party on July 4th at 9pm at Kilowatt in SF's Mission, presented by SFDJ Collective and Tumbaos to bring the neighborhood out for the holiday.

music.name: Project X: 2010s Dance Party
music.why: Nonstop 2010s banger playlist at DNA Lounge — festival drops, viral hits and hiphop anthems for the throwback crowd
music.summary: Project X: 2010s Dance Party at DNA Lounge in SF (375 11th St) on Saturday July 4, 9:30pm–2:30am, with DJs Jake D, BB Carolz and Lazyboy playing trap, hip-hop, pop, throwbacks, indie, house and R&B from 2010–2019; 18+, $10–$22.

music.name: Queen Out Pride w/ Tiffany Pollard, Prosperity Room
music.why: Campy pop-and-Pride club energy with a celebrity draw; for the dance-and-sing crowd, not purists
music.summary: A 21+ Pride-themed club night on Friday July 3, 9pm-3am, at Public Works in SF, blending pop and club sounds with a reality-TV personality appearance over the holiday weekend.

music.name: Quicksand: Sardonix, Robin So, YW, Future Sounds, Zero Gravity, Fyzn
music.why: Free renegade beach rave into the sunset — the kind of open-air day party worth chasing
music.summary: The Loud Mouths' free pop-up beach rave over July 4th weekend, running 3:45–10pm into the sunset with unlimited bass from Sardonix, Robin, So YW b2b Future Sounds, Zero Gravity, Fyzn plus open aux. RSVP on Partiful; the SF beach location is text-blasted to attendees at midnight 7/3.

music.name: Red, White, and Groove: Alissa, Haux, Vuskiii, DJ Genetixx
music.why: Cheap poolside afternoon house in Sacramento; the swimsuit-and-cocktail call, done by 8pm
music.summary: A 21+ July 4th pool party at Faces Nightclub in Sacramento, 4-8pm, with four DJs spinning house, minimal and tech for an afternoon of dancing and lounging poolside.

music.name: Roddy Lima, Weska
music.why: Big-room tech-house at a proper downtown club; the polished Friday-night option before the holiday
music.summary: A 21+ tech-house and techno nightclub show on Friday July 3 at 10pm at 1015 Folsom in SF, pairing two DJs for a late-night set at the multi-room downtown club.

music.name: Sinego (Timbales DJ Set)
music.why: A timbales live-percussion DJ set — actual drums, not just mixing; the most distinctive Saturday late-night format
music.summary: Sinego plays a timbales DJ set at Madarae in SF on Saturday July 4, 9pm–3am, presented by All I Need Events; Latin percussion, reggaeton and percussive club sounds; $14–$28.

music.name: The Gallery: A Renaissance of Sound (4th of July)
music.why: $15 all-night 4th-of-July house-and-minimal BBQ in an art space — cover at the door, no ticket needed
music.summary: A 4th of July 'Community. Art. Music.' party at The Gallery (257 3rd St, SoMa) running 5pm-2am, with a BBQ and a local house/minimal-techno lineup — Yango, Mode Leeloo, Silent J, Lox, Kal and Freddy Peaches; $15 cover at the door.

music.name: Yerba Buena Gardens Festival: Ricardo Lemvo & Makina Loca
music.address: Mission St between 3rd & 4th, San Francisco, CA 94103
music.why: Free outdoor Afro-Cuban salsa in the middle of downtown SF on a Thursday — picnic-and-dance accessible
music.summary: The Yerba Buena Gardens Festival presents Ricardo Lemvo & Makina Loca on Thursday July 2 at 6pm in Yerba Buena Gardens (Mission St between 3rd & 4th, SF), with live Afro-Cuban salsa; free, all ages.

music.name: Amor Digital Summer Jam
music.why: Intimate Oakland debut for a crew doing longer sets of diverse Latin American and Caribbean sounds, at a communal space
music.summary: Amor Digital returns to Oakland at Tamarack (community space and library) on Friday July 3, 9pm–2am, with DJs Juanny and Javi playing longer sets of Latin American and Caribbean electronic sounds; $6–$11. Zero tolerance for bigotry.

music.name: Joshua Slone: Thinking Too Much Tour
music.why: All-ages folk-pop show at The Fillmore — a sit-down touring act between the holiday dance parties
music.summary: Joshua Slone plays The Fillmore in San Francisco on Tuesday July 1, doors 7:30pm, show 8pm, on his 'Thinking Too Much' tour. All ages; $24.

music.name: Kelly McFarling
music.why: An intimate indie-folk songwriter night at The Independent — the quiet counterpoint to a holiday weekend full of raves
music.summary: Kelly McFarling plays The Independent in SF (628 Divisadero) on Thursday July 2, doors 7:30pm, show 8pm, with Rose Paradise and Uwade as support; all ages, $41.

music.name: Lindy in the Park – Swing Dance Party
music.address: JFK Drive, Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, CA 94118
music.why: Free weekly swing dance on car-free JFK Drive — beginners welcome, live music sometimes, no partner required
music.summary: Lindy in the Park is a free weekly swing and Lindy Hop social dance on Sunday July 5 at 11am on JFK Drive in Golden Gate Park near the de Young Museum, when the road is car-free; all ages and skill levels welcome.

music.name: Pride Decompression w/ Maja Blast!
music.why: The post-Pride wind-down house night for those who haven't stopped dancing yet
music.summary: A Pride Decompression house night on Wednesday July 2 at 9pm at Cat Club in SF (1190 Folsom), headlined by Maja Blast!; 21+, $10–$20 cover.

music.name: On Set (Isolator SF)
music.why: Cheap Friday techno at Underground SF's Haight Street basement — Isolator's underground residency, tiny and loud
music.summary: OnSet, Isolator SF's Friday techno night at Underground SF (424 Haight St), returns July 3 at 9pm with DJs Rubidium and Peter Clarke; $10.

music.name: Interzone Darkwave Tuesdays
music.why: A themed CyberGoth ritual night at F8 — stompy sirens and dark-drink specials, not a generic goth night
music.summary: Interzone's special 'Neon Sanctum' edition on Tuesday June 30, 9pm–2am, at F8 in SF (1192 Folsom). DJs KAJE, Hopelesss and Byter play dark techno and CyberGoth sounds; 21+, $5 after 10pm.

music.name: Function 500: ZZ + JMO
music.why: Eight-dollar Thursday techno at Public Works — underground local pairing, zero hype, all dance floor
music.summary: Function 500 presents ZZ and JMO for a Thursday techno night at Public Works in SF (161 Erie St) on July 2 at 9pm; $8.

music.name: Inner Circle ft. Tie Dye, Bastian Bell & Nita Jameau
music.why: Free house and disco night with a CDMX guest and creative remixes — no cover, low-key vibes at White Rabbit
music.summary: Inner Circle at White Rabbit in SF on Thursday July 2 at 9pm, headlined by Tie Dye with guests Bastian Bell and Nita Jameau (from Mexico City) plus Hoochi; house, disco and remixes; free.

music.name: Proper Tings: UKG, Dubstep & DnB
music.why: World Cup Edition UKG/dubstep/DnB night — the rare bass-music mid-week option in SF
music.summary: Proper Tings 'World Cup Edition' at O'Reilly's Pub in SF on Wednesday July 1, 9pm–2am. A UK garage, dubstep and drum & bass night; $10.

music.name: Oksional First Thursdays
music.why: Free monthly residency that gives talented local DJs extended headline slots they rarely get otherwise
music.summary: Oksional First Thursdays at Mothership in SF on Thursday July 2 at 8pm, featuring Amatric in an extended headline set with Justyn Myers in support, playing house and EBM; free.

music.name: Future Perfect Techno Kissa
music.why: Collaborative live techno performance series, East Bay edition — four live sets plus a final three-musician jam; intimate and free
music.summary: Future Perfect East Bay Edition at Spire (The Church) in Oakland on Sunday July 5, 6–10pm, featuring RMNA, All That's Gone and additional live performers in the Techno Kissa collaborative format; free.

music.name: Sindustry Sundays: 4th of July – NILE, Jeff Straw
music.why: Free Sunday sax-and-disco closer at Butter — DJ Jeff Straw live on sax, no cover to end the weekend right
music.summary: Sindustry Sundays at Butter in SF (354 11th St) on Sunday July 5, 9pm–2am, with resident DJ/saxophonist Jeff Straw and guest NILE; house and disco; free.

music.name: Treasure Island After Party
music.why: Secret-guest after-party at Audio continuing from the Elderbrook day party — ideal for anyone who can't stop
music.summary: The official Treasure Island day party after-party at Audio SF (316 11th St) on Friday July 3, 9:30pm–2am, with a secret US guest; 21+, $20.

sports.name: Alameda July 4th Parade R.A.C.E. (5K)
sports.why: Run the parade route before the parade rolls; the family July 4th tradition, not a PR chase
sports.summary: A family-friendly July 4th 5K run/walk starting 8:30am along the Alameda parade route, ending around 11am, timed to set up the town's Independence Day parade.

sports.name: Hercules Dynamite 4th of July 5K
sports.why: Tiny waterfront Bay Trail 5K capped at 250; intimate and kid-free-friendly, not a big-crowd race
sports.summary: A chip-timed July 4th 5K run/walk at 9am along the San Francisco Bay Trail from Hercules to Rodeo and back, capped at 250 entrants, with free entry for kids 10 and under.

sports.name: July 4th Run 5K/10K/13.1
sports.address: Seal Point Park, San Mateo, CA 94401
sports.why: Pick your distance up to a half marathon; the option for runners who actually want mileage on the 4th
sports.summary: A July 4th morning road race at Seal Point Park in San Mateo starting 7:30am, offering 1-mile, 5K, 10K and half-marathon distances for runners and walkers of every level.

sports.name: Kenwood Footrace 10K & 3K
sports.address: Warm Springs Rd & Plaza Park, Kenwood, CA 95452
sports.why: Run through wine-country vineyards then roll into a small-town July 4th parade; the most scenic race option
sports.summary: A July 4th 10K and 3K road race at 7:30am through Sonoma Valley vineyards and rolling hills from Plaza Park in Kenwood, drawing competitive runners and families, followed by the town parade, pancake breakfast and Rotary shindig.

sports.name: SF Bike Party #168
sports.why: A 250th-anniversary-of-America themed monthly mass ride through SF — costumes expected, free, leave no trace
sports.summary: SF Bike Party #168 'America $2.50' meets at Civic Center Plaza on Friday July 3 at 7:30pm with rollout at 8pm; a free, all-welcome themed mass bike ride through SF celebrating the 250th anniversary of America.

sports.name: Stars & Stripes 5K
sports.why: A low-key East Bay 5K close to home; the easy local July 4th jog, nothing fancy
sports.summary: A July 4th 5K road race starting at 8am in Concord, a small (100-500 runner) East Bay Independence Day run/walk on a flat road course welcoming participants of every level and pace.

sports.name: Sunset Dunes parkrun 5K
sports.address: 1349 Upper Great Hwy, San Francisco, CA 94122
sports.why: Free, no-bib, oceanfront 5K on a car-free highway; the casual no-commitment morning move
sports.summary: A free, weekly timed 5K at 8am along the car-free Upper Great Highway at Sunset Dunes Park in SF, open to runners and walkers of all levels, no race entry required.

sports.name: Bay 2 Bankers 5K/10K
sports.address: 900 North Point St, San Francisco, CA 94109
sports.why: The 14th running of a mystery-themed navigational race from Ghirardelli through SF classics — not a regular road course
sports.summary: The Bay 2 Bankers 5K/10K on Saturday July 4 at 9am starts at Ghirardelli Square (900 North Point St, SF); teams of 1–5 use a custom map to navigate checkpoints through classic SF neighborhoods; $24. Post-race refreshments included.

sports.name: Blue Heron 5K
sports.address: Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, CA
sports.why: USATF-certified Golden Gate Park 5K on the Fourth — flat, scenic, and properly timed with a race bib
sports.summary: The Blue Heron 5K on Saturday July 4 at 8am at Blue Heron Lake in Golden Gate Park (SF); a USATF-certified road race through the park on Independence Day; $50.

sports.name: Berkeley Running Club: Tilden Park Trail Run
sports.why: Free group trail run in Tilden Park every Tuesday — the best gateway into East Bay hills running
sports.summary: Berkeley Running Club hosts a free Tuesday evening trail run on July 1 at 6:30pm starting at Lone Oak in Tilden Park (199 Loop Rd, Berkeley); open to all paces and experience levels.

sports.name: Fog City Run (Wednesday 5K)
sports.why: A timed but low-key $5 weekly 5K from a Union St bar — faster than parkrun, looser than a race
sports.summary: Fog City Run is a weekly timed 5K on Wednesday July 2 at 6:30pm starting at The Blue Light (1979 Union St, SF); $5 race fee, rain or shine.

sports.name: Marin Running Club: Tuesday Bunny Run
sports.address: 703 Tennessee Valley Rd, Mill Valley, CA 94941
sports.why: Free beginner-friendly trail run in the Marin Headlands every Tuesday — ideal intro to Marin trails
sports.summary: Marin Running Club hosts a free Tuesday trail run on July 1 at 6pm, starting at Tennessee Valley Trailhead/Miwok Stables Parking Lot (703 Tennessee Valley Rd, Mill Valley); beginner and intermediate levels welcome.

sports.name: Mission 5K Fun Run
sports.why: Free neighborhood run-bar crawl through the Mission every Monday — social running, not race pace
sports.summary: SF Mission Fun Runs hosts a free social 5K on Monday June 29 at 6:30pm, starting and ending at Teeth Bar (2323 Mission St, SF), with a rotating route through Mission bars and cafes; free, all paces welcome.

sports.name: Oakland Beer Runners: Lake Merritt Run
sports.why: Casual Oakland beer run with zero time pressure — social miles around the lake, then drinks after
sports.summary: Oakland Beer Runners meets at Original Pattern Brewing Co. in Oakland on Tuesday July 1 at 6:30pm for a casual 2–6 mile run around Lake Merritt with post-run beers; free, no timing.

sports.name: Polo Field 5K (DSE Runners)
sports.address: Middle Dr W & Metson Rd, San Francisco, CA
sports.why: Free community Sunday 5K at the Polo Fields with DSE Runners — the no-bib, show-up-and-go option
sports.summary: DSE Runners hosts a free community 5K on Sunday July 5 at 9am at the Golden Gate Park Polo Fields (Middle Dr W & Metson Rd, SF); open to all, no registration or timing chip required.

sports.name: SFRRC Wednesday Fun Run in the Presidio
sports.address: 610 Old Mason St, San Francisco, CA 94129
sports.why: Free Wednesday group run through the Presidio — SF's oldest running club, all paces, great views
sports.summary: San Francisco Road Runners Club hosts a free Wednesday group run on July 2 at 6pm starting at Sports Basement Presidio (610 Old Mason St, SF); open to all levels and paces through Presidio trails.
